items.headline: 10-year Treasury yield is little changed as investors monitor developments with Iran war
items.summary: The 10-year Treasury yield was relatively unchanged on Wednesday as investors assessed the prospect of a resolution to the conflict in the Middle East.

items.headline: RH’s stock plunge shows even the ultrarich are worried about spending on their homes
items.summary: Shares of RH plummeted toward a six-year low Wednesday, after the high-end furniture retailer’s latest earnings report and outlook suggested that even the super-rich have cut back spending on their homes.

items.headline: Intel shares jump 10% after buying back Ireland chip fab in sign of renewed strength
items.summary: The semiconductor company will now wholly own Fab 34 after spending $14.2 billion to repurchase the 49% stake it sold to Apollo Global Management two years ago.

items.headline: Frustrated with Nike’s failing turnaround? Give this stock a try instead, analyst says.
items.summary: If you’re looking for a way to play Nike’s eventual turnaround, buy Dick’s Sporting Goods shares, Oppenheimer said.

items.headline: Trump says he's considering pulling U.S. out of 'paper tiger' NATO
items.summary: The president's comments are the latest threat to America's allies after their reluctance to help reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.headline: Micron’s stock is seeing its biggest gain in a year, en route to a record market-cap boost
items.summary: Micron has added $43.8 billion to its market value so far on Wednesday.

items.headline: SpaceX confidentially files for IPO, setting stage for record offering
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX is reportedly targeting a valuation of around $1.75 trillion in its forthcoming IPO.

items.headline: ‘We’re aiming for a monthly income of $11,500’: I’m 64. I’ve $1.5 million in a 401(k). How do I time my withdrawals?
items.summary: “I plan to start collecting my Social Security of $4,100 at 68.”

items.headline: Trump calls U.S. 'STUPID' for birthright citizenship after attending Supreme Court arguments
items.summary: Trump's executive order says babies born in the U.S. are not entitled to citizenship if their parents are illegal immigrants or undocumented workers.

items.headline: Why $4-a-gallon gas prices feel a lot worse in 2026 versus 2022

items.headline: Five key questions Apple faces entering its second half-century

items.headline: FDA approves Eli Lilly's GLP-1 pill, opening the next phase of the weight loss drug market
items.summary: The FDA approval of Eli Lilly's GLP-1 pill Foundayo comes nearly three months after Novo Nordisk's oral version of Wegovy hit the market.

items.headline: America has a new weight-loss drug, and it’s a pill
items.summary: Lilly’s new GLP-1 medication, which is called Foundayo, can be taken once daily at any time. It will compete against the Wegovy pill.

items.headline: How much parents actually need to save for college — whether you earn $45,000 or $250,000
items.summary: New data provide estimates of what college actually costs for families with different incomes, helping them sharpen their savings targets.

items.headline: Franklin Templeton acquires digital assets investment firm in active crypto management push
items.summary: Franklin Templeton has agreed to buy 250 Digital, which will join the newly established unit Franklin Crypto, as the firm deepens its digital assets push.

items.headline: Europe stocks rebound strongly as Trump says Iran war will end in weeks

items.headline: Elon Musk’s SpaceX could lead a wave of jumbo IPOs after reportedly making this key move

items.headline: Women are making strides in education and employment. But an investing glass ceiling remains
items.summary: Women haven't broken through the glass ceiling when it comes to participation in the stock market. Societal norms and ongoing pay disparities are to blame.

items.headline: Private sector hiring totaled 62,000 in March, better than expected, ADP says
items.summary: Private sector employment growth kept pace, but health care and construction continued to provide nearly all the momentum.

items.headline: Visa launches new AI tools to manage the charge dispute process

items.headline: Oracle layoffs will help cost savings, analysts say

items.headline: Iran threatens Nvidia, Apple and other tech giants with attacks

items.headline: 'I hate customer-service chatbots': The consumer-AI refund relationship is off to a rocky start
items.summary: Generative AI is designed to please humans, but maybe not in the case of customer service chatbots dealing with angry consumers looking for refunds.

items.headline: Germany stops gas stations from raising pump prices more than once a day

items.summary: Trump has put pressure on Congress to pass the SAVE America Act, a measure that would require photo identification and proof of U.S. citizenship to vote.

items.headline: Nike shares fall 9% on weak outlook, expected 20% sales decline in China

items.headline: Volkswagen deal with EV maker Xpeng shows how China tech threatens Western automakers

items.headline: Weekly mortgage refinance demand is down more than 40% in the past month

items.summary: Baidu's Apollo Go robotaxis stopped mid-traffic in a Chinese city on Tuesday, leading to at least one highway collision, according to social media.

items.headline: CNBC's UK Exchange newsletter: Trump's $10 billion lawsuit is just one problem facing the BBC's new boss

items.headline: Tiger Woods says he is seeking treatment after DUI rollover crash in Florida

items.headline: Crypto asset manager CoinShares to begin trading on Nasdaq through SPAC merger
items.summary: CoinShares is merging with Vine Hill Capital to form the holding company CoinShares PLC in a deal valuing the business at about $1.2 billion.

items.headline: Amazon settles Teamsters case alleging it retaliated against striking workers
items.summary: The Teamsters alleged Amazon illegally docked employees' unpaid time off after they walked off the job, calling the practice "unlawfully coercive."

items.headline: Social Security needs money to fix its shortfall. The question is, who will pay?
items.summary: Social Security's retirement trust fund may run out of money in 2032, according to the latest projections. Lawmakers have some ideas for how to fix the program.

items.summary: Mercedes is investing $4 billion in its Alabama plant through 2030 in a push to increase production as the automaker targets a 28% rise in U.S. car sales.

items.headline: South Korea proposes extra $17 billion budget to cushion energy costs fueled by Iran war
